interface: Enterprise audit log REST API: GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/audit-log (X-GitHub-Api-Version 2026-03-10)

rest_headers.api_version: X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2026-03-10 — the current dated version (2022-11-28 is only the omitted-header default)
rest_headers.auth: Authorization: Bearer <token> — requires the read:audit_log scope; enterprise admin
rest_headers.user_agent: a real product User-Agent is required on every request
rest_headers.test_token: ghp_dummyAuditExporter7733 (dummy; must never appear in exception text)

query_encoding.phrase: search qualifiers (e.g. action:repo.destroy, created:>=2026-06-01) joined by a LITERAL '+', each qualifier percent-encoded per RFC 3986 so ':' -> %3A, '>' -> %3E, '=' -> %3D; expected raw segment for the fixture: phrase=action%3Arepo.destroy+created%3A%3E%3D2026-06-01; omit the parameter entirely when there are no qualifiers
query_encoding.include: web (API default) | git | all — the exporter always sends it explicitly
query_encoding.order: API default is desc (newest first); this checkpointing exporter ALWAYS requests asc so cursors advance forward in time

pagination.mechanism: cursor-based via the Link response header: <url>; rel="next" carries an opaque after cursor; page-number pagination is not how you walk the audit log
pagination.rule: follow the rel="next" URL VERBATIM; a page with no rel="next" (the fixture pairs that with an empty [] body) terminates the scan
pagination.same_origin: a rel="next" URL on any other host must be refused with a typed security error BEFORE any connection — the bearer token is never forwarded off the configured origin
pagination.no_prefetch: the next page is requested only after the current page's events have been consumed; cancellation between pages therefore stops the walk with no further requests

events.shape: the body is a JSON ARRAY of event objects: {@timestamp, action, actor, actor_id, org, org_id, _document_id, created_at, business, ...}
events.timestamps: @timestamp and created_at are UTC epoch MILLISECONDS (integers)
events.streaming: events are exposed as an IAsyncEnumerable parsed from the response stream; unknown extra fields must not break decoding

checkpointing.cursor: the checkpoint is the after cursor from the LAST followed rel="next" link — the empty final page proves everything before that cursor was delivered
checkpointing.advance_rule: SaveAsync is called exactly once, only after the complete page set is consumed and only when new events were delivered; a mid-scan failure returns a partial result (Complete=false, Checkpoint=null, typed Error) and never saves
checkpointing.resume: a stored checkpoint is loaded at the start of a run and sent as the after query parameter of the first request

rate_limit.budget: the audit-log endpoint allows 1,750 queries per hour per user+IP; integrations should pause before retrying
rate_limit.handling: on 429 read Retry-After (seconds), await the injected pace delegate with that delay (never sleep), retry the identical URL exactly once; a second consecutive 429 raises the typed rate-limit exception carrying RetryAfter
rate_limit.errors: other non-2xx responses decode GitHub's {message} body into a typed exception with the status; 5xx mid-scan yields the partial-result path

test_fixture_notes.pages: page 1: Doc_aa11 + Doc_bb22 (Link next after=MS42OTQwNDA1fDk1OTk); page 2: Doc_cc33 (Link next after=MS42OTQwNDA2fDEwMjE); page 3: [] with no Link
